_note: Restaurant names and addresses sourced from Google Maps. Please verify hours, phone numbers, and other details directly with each restaurant before visiting.

cities.name: Toronto
cities.description: Canada's largest city and home to one of the biggest Filipino communities, centred in Scarborough and Mississauga.

cities.name: Vancouver
cities.description: A vibrant Filipino community with excellent restaurants spread across the Lower Mainland.

cities.name: Montréal
cities.description: A growing Filipino food scene in Canada's cultural capital.

cities.name: Calgary
cities.description: Alberta's largest city with a thriving Filipino community and a growing restaurant scene.

cities.name: Kelowna
cities.description: A tight-knit Filipino food community in the beautiful Okanagan.

cities.name: Winnipeg
cities.description: One of the highest concentrations of Filipino Canadians per capita — and the food scene shows it.

cities.name: Edmonton
cities.description: A large and well-established Filipino community with excellent authentic restaurants.

cities.name: Ottawa
cities.description: The nation's capital has a close-knit Filipino community with charming home-style gems.

restaurants.name: Lola Asty's Authentic Filipino Cuisine
restaurants.blurb: Scarborough's highest-rated Filipino gem — Lola Asty's home-style cooking earns its legendary reputation.
restaurants.description: Lola Asty's is the name on every Scarborough Filipino foodie's lips. With a near-perfect rating, this home-style restaurant delivers the kind of sinigang and kare-kare that tastes like it came straight from a grandmother's kitchen. Every dish is made with care, and the loyal regulars wouldn't have it any other way. Expect a warm, welcoming atmosphere where you're treated like family from the moment you walk in.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The highest-rated Filipino restaurant in the GTA for good reason — Lola Asty's sinigang broth is the gold standard, perfectly sour and deeply fragrant. Regulars come from all over Scarborough and beyond.

restaurants.name: Manyaman Foods Filipino Cuisine
restaurants.blurb: Manyaman means delicious in Ilocano, and this Kennedy Road turo-turo lives up to every letter of that name.
restaurants.description: Manyaman Foods on Kennedy Road is one of Scarborough's best-kept secrets for authentic Filipino turo-turo cooking. The steam table is always full of rotating daily specials — adobo, sinigang, pakbet, and more — all cooked in generous portions and served over rice. The homely atmosphere and consistently excellent food keep regulars coming back daily.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The rotating daily menu means there is always something new to try, but the adobo and kare-kare are always present and always perfect. Outstanding value in the heart of Scarborough.

restaurants.name: LAKAY Kusina
restaurants.blurb: Lakay Kusina brings the bold, rustic flavours of traditional Filipino home cooking to Ellesmere Road.
restaurants.description: LAKAY Kusina cooks the way grandparents cook — with patience, seasoned pots, and no shortcuts. The dinuguan is rich and properly funky, and the sinigang is consistently on point. This Ellesmere Road spot is a neighbourhood staple where you come for lunch and end up staying for a snack.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The dinuguan at LAKAY is among the best in Scarborough — a deeply flavoured, savoury stew that will convert the uninitiated. Don't miss it.

restaurants.name: J & J Kapuso Filipino Restaurant
restaurants.blurb: Kapuso means close to the heart — and J&J has been exactly that for the Scarborough Filipino community.
restaurants.description: J&J Kapuso has carved out a loyal following on Ellesmere Road for its honest Filipino cooking and welcoming service. The lechon and adobo are the headline acts, drawing repeat visitors who value consistency above all else. The LGBTQ+-friendly environment adds to the warmth of this community-oriented spot.
restaurants.whyFavorite: One of the friendliest Filipino restaurants in Scarborough, J&J Kapuso always makes you feel at home. The pancit is a crowd favourite and the portions are generous.

restaurants.name: Pinoy Halo Halo
restaurants.blurb: As the name promises, the halo-halo here is the real deal — and the Filipino daily specials are a reliable bonus.
restaurants.description: Pinoy Halo Halo puts the beloved Filipino dessert front and centre, serving towering, generous portions loaded with ube, leche flan, kaong, and shaved ice. Beyond dessert, the restaurant offers a selection of Filipino mains — adobo, sinigang, and pancit — making it a full-service spot for a Filipino food fix in Scarborough.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The halo-halo is the star attraction — perfectly balanced between sweet, creamy, and icy. Come with friends and order a round for the table after a Filipino lunch.

restaurants.name: Manyaman Foods Express
restaurants.blurb: The Passmore express location of the beloved Manyaman Foods — fast, delicious, and always satisfying.
restaurants.description: Manyaman Foods Express on Passmore serves the northern Scarborough Filipino community with a quick-service model that does not sacrifice quality. The lumpia Shanghai is crispy and packed with flavour, and the adobo rice bowls are the go-to lunch. Efficient, affordable, and consistently good — exactly what a Filipino lunch spot should be.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The lumpia here is among the crispiest and most flavourful in Scarborough. Perfect for a quick, satisfying Filipino lunch on a busy weekday.

restaurants.name: Klasik Inasal and Noodle Express
restaurants.blurb: Vancouver's top-rated Filipino spot on East Broadway — the inasal-inspired flavours and noodle dishes are knockout.
restaurants.description: Klasik Inasal and Noodle Express brings the smoky, tangy tradition of Bacolod-style inasal to Mount Pleasant in a fast, affordable format. The noodle dishes are a highlight — from batchoy to pancit — while the mains include hearty Filipino classics. With a 4.8-star rating, it is one of the most consistently praised Filipino restaurants in Vancouver.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The highest-rated Filipino restaurant on East Broadway. The pancit dishes are made fresh to order and the inasal chicken has that perfect garlic-vinegar-annatto marinade.

restaurants.name: HALINA Modern Filipino Kitchen + Bar
restaurants.blurb: HALINA is Vancouver's most exciting Filipino restaurant — modern, vibrant, and deeply delicious.
restaurants.description: HALINA is the invitation to experience Filipino cuisine at its modern best. Located on East Hastings, this kitchen and bar brings bold, contemporary Filipino cooking to Vancouver's vibrant east side. The kare-kare and sisig draw rave reviews, and the cocktail program takes inspiration from Filipino ingredients. Reservations strongly recommended.
restaurants.whyFavorite: HALINA is the restaurant that is converting Vancouverites into Filipino food fans. The kare-kare with oxtail is revelatory, and the atmosphere is electric on weekend evenings.

restaurants.name: Kulinarya Filipino Eatery
restaurants.blurb: Kulinarya on the Drive brings traditional Filipino cooking to Vancouver's most eclectic food street.
restaurants.description: On Commercial Drive — Vancouver's most diverse food corridor — Kulinarya Filipino Eatery holds its own among the competition with a menu of lovingly prepared Filipino classics. The sinigang is a standout, and the kare-kare with homemade bagoong is a must-order. The warm, attentive service and inviting space make it ideal for groups and date nights alike.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The kare-kare at Kulinarya is one of the best in Vancouver — rich, peanut-forward, and served with perfectly funky bagoong. A Commercial Drive gem.

restaurants.name: Romeo Must Taste
restaurants.blurb: With a name this charming, Romeo Must Taste on E Hastings has no trouble winning over Vancouver diners.
restaurants.description: Romeo Must Taste is a neighbourhood Filipino favourite on the East Side with a menu that covers all the comfort-food classics. The adobo and sinigang are reliable crowd-pleasers, and the lechon kawali has a crackle that will make you reach for more. The casual, cheerful setting and honest prices make it a regular stop for locals.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Romeo Must Taste delivers consistently good Filipino food at fair prices. The sinigang is spot-on sour and the lechon kawali is gloriously crispy every time.

restaurants.name: Kimshaker Resto: Modern Filipino Cuisine
restaurants.blurb: Burnaby's modern Filipino dining room where Kimshaker reinterprets beloved classics with contemporary flair.
restaurants.description: Kimshaker Resto sits on the busy Kingsway corridor and offers a modern take on Filipino cuisine that attracts both the Filipino community and curious food adventurers. The sisig comes sizzling and the kare-kare showcases premium ingredients. With a clean, contemporary dining space and creative presentations, Kimshaker is Burnaby's Filipino dining destination.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Kimshaker's sisig is the most creative version in Burnaby — crispy, balanced, and full of that signature savoury-sour character. The modern space is great for groups.

restaurants.name: Buboy
restaurants.blurb: Montreal's buzziest Filipino spot — Buboy is a nickname turned restaurant, and it delivers bold unforgettable flavours.
restaurants.description: Buboy has quickly become the name in Montreal Filipino dining. Named affectionately after a common Filipino nickname, this downtown spot serves unapologetically bold Filipino food — rich kare-kare, sizzling sisig, and perfectly tangy sinigang. The intimate space fills quickly, especially on weekends, so arrive early or plan ahead.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Montreal's best Filipino restaurant earns that title through consistency and bold flavour. The adobo here is the definitive version — dark, vinegary, and deeply savoury. A must-visit on any Montreal food tour.

restaurants.name: Kalye Restaurant
restaurants.blurb: Kalye — street in Filipino — brings the vibrant soul of Philippine street food to Montreal's Cote-des-Neiges.
restaurants.description: Kalye Restaurant captures the energy and flavour of Philippine street food culture in the heart of Cote-des-Neiges. From crispy, golden lumpia to a halo-halo dessert that stops conversations in their tracks, every dish is made with intention. The warm, convivial atmosphere makes it an ideal spot for a leisurely Filipino feast with friends.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Kalye's halo-halo is spectacular — a proper tower of shaved ice, ube, leche flan, and tropical sweetness. One of the best Filipino dessert experiences in Canada.

restaurants.name: Cuisine De Manille
restaurants.blurb: Cuisine De Manille brings the soul of Manila's most beloved dishes to Montreal's Filipino community.
restaurants.description: Cuisine De Manille is one of Montreal's most established Filipino restaurants, serving the Cote-des-Neiges community for years with consistent, heartfelt cooking. The lechon and kare-kare are highlights, and the pancit noodle dishes are made fresh. A trusted neighbourhood spot that puts quality and community first.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Cuisine De Manille's lechon is the standout dish — crispy skin, tender meat, and served with a side of liver sauce that elevates every bite.

restaurants.name: The J's Restaurant & Bakery
restaurants.blurb: Calgary's crown jewel — The J's combines an excellent Filipino kitchen with a beloved bakery in one incredible stop.
restaurants.description: The J's Restaurant & Bakery is Calgary's highest-rated Filipino establishment and for outstanding reason. The restaurant side delivers exceptional lechon, adobo, and sinigang made from family recipes, while the bakery produces Filipino breads and pastries that have their own loyal following. The combination makes it a one-stop destination for the full Filipino food experience.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The J's is perfect from start to dessert — excellent Filipino mains followed by freshly baked Filipino pastries from the bakery counter. The lechon here has perfect crackling skin every single time.

restaurants.name: Amihan Grill + Bakeshop Downtown
restaurants.blurb: Amihan brings the freshest Filipino grill flavours to downtown Calgary, with a killer bakeshop counter to match.
restaurants.description: Amihan Grill + Bakeshop's downtown Calgary location puts Filipino food at the heart of the city's core. The grilled dishes are seasoned with care and the lechon is a consistent standout. The bakeshop side offers Filipino breads and pastries that make it a morning and afternoon destination too. Clean, welcoming space that works for business lunches and family dinners alike.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Having Amihan downtown means Filipino food is always accessible in the city centre. The halo-halo is a must for dessert, and the Filipino pastries are fresh daily.

restaurants.name: Kalesa Grill
restaurants.blurb: Kalesa Grill is a Filipino grilling institution in NE Calgary, celebrated for its sizzling sisig and smoky meats.
restaurants.description: Named after the traditional horse-drawn carriage of the Philippines, Kalesa Grill carries a sense of cultural pride in every dish. The sisig is sizzling, crispy, and utterly addictive. The kare-kare with slow-braised oxtail is another highlight that keeps the dining room full on weekends.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Kalesa's sisig is the benchmark for Filipino sisig in Calgary — aggressively seasoned, crispy-edged, and served sizzling hot. One of the most celebrated dishes in the city's Filipino restaurant scene.

restaurants.name: Amihan Grill + Bakeshop Macleod Trail
restaurants.blurb: The beloved south Calgary Amihan location serving Macleod Trail with the same excellent grilled Filipino fare.
restaurants.address: 7516 Macleod Trl SE Unit 140, Calgary, AB
restaurants.description: The Macleod Trail location of Amihan Grill + Bakeshop has been a fixture in south Calgary's Filipino community. The same quality menu from the downtown location — excellent lechon, fresh-baked Filipino breads, and a rotating selection of grilled and braised dishes — served in the heart of the Southland Filipino neighbourhood.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Amihan Macleod is the neighbourhood anchor for south Calgary Filipino families. The pancit and lechon are the go-to orders, and the bakeshop pastries sell out fast on weekends.

restaurants.name: Tambayan at Kainang Filipino Restaurant
restaurants.blurb: Tambayan at Kainang is the community hangout — affordable, generous, and deeply comforting Filipino cooking.
restaurants.description: Tambayan means hangout spot and Kainang means dining — and this Calgary spot is exactly that for the Filipino community. The casual, welcoming place where families gather for weekday meals and Sunday feasts. The bulalo is a highlight — slow-simmered bone marrow broth with corn and cabbage that warms you from the inside out.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The bulalo at Tambayan is the best in Calgary — rich, deeply flavoured, and served in massive portions that justify the trip. The adobo rice is the ideal companion.

restaurants.name: Drew's Kapampangan Cuisine Ltd.
restaurants.blurb: Winnipeg's crown jewel — Drew's Kapampangan Cuisine brings the bold flavours of Pampanga to the prairies.
restaurants.description: Kapampangan cuisine — from the province of Pampanga, considered the culinary heartland of the Philippines — has its finest prairie ambassador in Drew's. The sisig here is the authentic Kapampangan original: chopped pork face, liver, and chilis grilled on a hot plate. The kare-kare and dinuguan are prepared with the deep flavour complexity that defines this proud regional cuisine.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Drew's is the best Filipino restaurant in Winnipeg by unanimous vote of the community. The Kapampangan sisig here is irreplaceable — no other restaurant in the prairies does it this authentically.

restaurants.name: Casa Ilocandia
restaurants.blurb: Casa Ilocandia is a rare gem — one of Canada's only restaurants specializing in the distinct cuisine of Ilocos Norte.
restaurants.description: Casa Ilocandia brings the distinct culinary heritage of Ilocos Norte to Winnipeg's Sargent Avenue. Ilocano cooking is known for its vegetable-forward dishes, vinegar-heavy flavours, and the use of fermented condiments. The dinengdeng — a fish and vegetable broth unique to the region — is a revelation. For diners unfamiliar with this regional cuisine, Casa Ilocandia is a true discovery.
restaurants.whyFavorite: There is almost nowhere else in Canada to taste authentic Ilocano cooking. Casa Ilocandia's dinengdeng and bagnet are a culinary education in one meal.

restaurants.name: Mar's Sisig Restaurant
restaurants.blurb: The name says it all — Mar's Sisig is Winnipeg's unmissable destination for the sizzling Filipino classic.
restaurants.description: Mar's Sisig Restaurant does exactly what it promises — serves exceptional sisig in a no-frills setting on McPhillips Street. The signature sisig is sizzling, properly crispy, and flavoured with just the right amount of chili heat. The supporting menu of adobo, lechon kawali, and pancit are equally well-executed. A neighbourhood legend in north Winnipeg.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Mar's sisig is the benchmark in north Winnipeg — always crackling, always perfectly seasoned. Come hungry and order the extra-large portion.

restaurants.name: Tito Boy Restaurant
restaurants.blurb: Tito Boy's is a St. Vital institution on St. Anne's Road — reliable, generous, and always full of families.
restaurants.address: 730 St Anne's Rd Unit M, Winnipeg, MB
restaurants.description: Tito Boy Restaurant has built a fiercely loyal following in St. Vital's Filipino community. The homey atmosphere makes you feel like a guest at a relative's house. The adobo and sinigang are made fresh daily, and the kare-kare with ox tripe is the dish regulars keep coming back for.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Tito Boy's feels like eating at a Filipino relative's home — warm, generous, and full of heart. The kare-kare here is outstanding, with a sauce that has real depth and richness.

restaurants.name: LaMesa Restaurant and Bar
restaurants.blurb: Edmonton's premier Filipino dining destination — LaMesa delivers elevated Filipino fare in a vibrant, welcoming space.
restaurants.description: LaMesa (the table in Filipino) is the gathering place for Edmonton's Filipino community and for adventurous food lovers alike. With a near-perfect rating, it earns its reputation through consistently excellent lechon, a remarkable kare-kare, and a sizzling sisig that rivals the best in Canada. The bar program adds a social dimension perfect for long, celebratory evenings.
restaurants.whyFavorite: LaMesa is Edmonton's must-visit Filipino restaurant. The lechon is carved table-side on weekends and the kare-kare with oxtail is rich, peanutty perfection. Book ahead — it fills up fast.

restaurants.name: Bernardo Diner
restaurants.blurb: Tucked inside Northgate Centre, Bernardo Diner is north Edmonton's beloved home-style Filipino comfort food spot.
restaurants.description: Bernardo Diner occupies a modest space inside Northgate Centre but punches well above its weight with some of north Edmonton's finest Filipino cooking. The bulalo is the signature — a rich bone broth soup with beef shank and marrow that takes hours to develop its deep flavour. The adobo and sinigang are equally dependable. A community institution with generations of loyal customers.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Bernardo's bulalo is the kind of bone broth that reminds you why slow-cooked food is worth the wait. Rich, clean, deeply comforting — it is north Edmonton's best kept Filipino secret.

restaurants.name: Kusina Filipino Take Out & Catering
restaurants.blurb: Edmonton's go-to for Filipino takeout and catering — Kusina cooks fresh, hearty, and always delicious every day.
restaurants.description: Kusina Filipino Take Out & Catering has become one of Edmonton's most trusted Filipino food sources for both daily takeout and large-scale catering. The menu rotates daily with fresh-cooked Filipino classics, and the catering service has delivered memorable fiesta spreads across the city. Simple presentation, exceptional flavour.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Kusina's kare-kare takeout container is the most satisfying Filipino takeout in central Edmonton. Perfect to bring to a potluck or enjoy at home on a cold Alberta evening.

restaurants.name: Basti's Kitchen
restaurants.blurb: Basti's Kitchen in northwest Edmonton cooks deeply traditional Filipino food in the most welcoming home-kitchen setting.
restaurants.description: Basti's Kitchen brings the warmth and tradition of Filipino home cooking to northwest Edmonton. The menu leans into the hearty, bold dishes that define Filipino comfort food — dinuguan with its rich, dark pork broth, kare-kare with proper annatto-colored peanut sauce, and an adobo that is clearly made from a well-tested family recipe.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Basti's dinuguan is the real deal — dark, earthy, and intensely flavoured. It is the kind of dish that non-Filipinos try once and immediately need to have again.

restaurants.name: Cebuchon & BBQ
restaurants.blurb: Cebuchon brings the legendary lechon tradition of Cebu to Edmonton — crispy, smoky, and utterly irresistible.
restaurants.description: Cebuchon & BBQ takes its name from Cebu's world-famous lechon tradition and honours it in every roast. The skin is crackle-perfect and the meat is seasoned from the inside with lemongrass and aromatics — the Cebuano way. The BBQ section adds grilled pork belly, chicken, and seafood skewers to the mix.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The Cebu-style lechon at Cebuchon is among the finest in Canada — the lemongrass-stuffed interior and hyper-crispy skin are the real thing. Order it for a group and watch it disappear.

restaurants.name: Lola's Kitchen Filipino Restaurant
restaurants.blurb: A Centretown institution — Lola's Kitchen cooks Filipino food with the love and warmth of a grandmother's home.
restaurants.description: Lola's Kitchen on Somerset Street has become one of Ottawa's most beloved Filipino restaurants by doing one thing exceptionally well: cooking with heart. The sinigang is perfectly calibrated and the kare-kare has an honest, rich peanut sauce that speaks of real technique and love. Named after lola (grandmother), every dish carries that nurturing quality.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Lola's Kitchen is the quintessential Ottawa Filipino experience. The sinigang has that perfect combination of sour, savoury, and fresh vegetable that makes it one of the best in Ontario.

restaurants.name: Divisoria Resto Bar
restaurants.blurb: Named after Manila's famous market district, Divisoria Resto Bar serves lively Filipino food in east Ottawa.
restaurants.description: Divisoria Resto Bar takes its name from the famous market in Manila known for its vibrant energy and variety — and the restaurant captures exactly that spirit. Located in Gloucester, it is east Ottawa's top Filipino dining destination, with a menu that covers the classics executed brilliantly. The sisig sizzles and the lechon crackling is legendary.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Divisoria's lechon is the talk of east Ottawa — impeccably roasted with that signature shatteringly crisp skin. Pair it with a house cocktail for the full experience.

restaurants.name: The PH Kitchen
restaurants.blurb: The PH Kitchen in Nepean brings modern Filipino flavours to Ottawa's west end with a growing loyal following.
restaurants.description: The PH Kitchen stands for Philippine Kitchen and it delivers on that identity with a menu that celebrates the full breadth of Filipino cooking. Located in Nepean, it serves west Ottawa's Filipino community with a mix of traditional favourites and creative updates. The kare-kare is rich and aromatic, and the halo-halo dessert is one of the best in the city.
restaurants.whyFavorite: The PH Kitchen's halo-halo is Ottawa's finest — a generous, beautifully layered dessert with excellent ube ice cream. The kare-kare is also a must-order for any first visit.

restaurants.name: Jeepney Republiq
restaurants.blurb: Named after the iconic Filipino jeepney, this Kelowna restaurant is the Okanagan's best Filipino destination.
restaurants.description: Jeepney Republiq is Kelowna's Filipino food landmark — named after the brightly painted public transit vehicles that are synonymous with the Philippines. The restaurant brings the full range of Filipino classics to the Okanagan, from sizzling sisig to celebratory lechon. The vibrant, colourful interior matches the food's bold personality.
restaurants.whyFavorite: Jeepney Republiq is the number one reason Filipino Canadians move to Kelowna without fear of missing the food. The sisig and lechon are outstanding, and the halo-halo makes for a glorious Okanagan summer dessert.

restaurants.name: PNB - Pretty Not Bad
restaurants.blurb: PNB delivers way beyond its humble name — the Filipino cooking here is genuinely excellent and deeply satisfying.
restaurants.description: Pretty Not Bad — PNB — is the kind of name that makes you smile before you even walk in. And the food rewards that warmth. Located on Clement Avenue, PNB serves a focused menu of Filipino classics with consistency and care. The sinigang is tangy and full of fresh vegetables, and the adobo has that deep, wine-dark colour that signals it has been cooked low and slow.
restaurants.whyFavorite: PNB's late closing time makes it Kelowna's best option for a Filipino dinner after a long day in the Okanagan sun. The kare-kare here is unexpectedly excellent — rich and properly fermented bagoong on the side.
